topic: A customer orders at a neighborhood coffee shop and chats briefly with the friendly barista about the menu — what's popular, a pastry recommendation, and whether to have it for here or to go.
title: Ordering at the Coffee Shop

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Hi, I'd like a coffee, but I'm not sure what to get.

translations.ko: 안녕하세요, 커피를 마시고 싶은데 뭘 주문할지 잘 모르겠어요.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Our cappuccino is really popular, and so is the cold brew.

translations.ko: 저희 카푸치노가 정말 인기가 많고, 콜드브루도 인기가 많아요.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Great choice! Would you like a pastry with that?

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: The blueberry muffins are fresh this morning, and they're delicious.

translations.ko: 블루베리 머핀이 오늘 아침에 나온 신선한 거고, 정말 맛있어요.

turns.speaker: A
turns.text: Perfect, I'll take one of those.

turns.speaker: B
turns.text: Sounds good! Is this for here or to go?
